morphology.summary: A compact semantic-control capsule for responsibility; morphology is not the focus of this seed entry.

etymology.summary: Etymology for responsibility is intentionally deferred in this semantic-control seed capsule.

semantic_drift.summary: Semantic-control usage focuses on present operational meaning rather than historical drift for responsibility.

present_usage.summary: Ownership of a duty, effect, or response without necessarily making a legal admission.

senses.definition: Ownership of a duty, effect, or response without necessarily making a legal admission.

examples.text: The word responsibility changes what claims the agent may safely make.

cultural_frame.summary: responsibility appears in communication where tone, risk, evidence, or commitment must be controlled.

frames.label: semantic control
frames.description: Frames responsibility as a term whose pragmatic force can change the safe output path.

pragmatics.summary: Responsibility language can signal accountability, care, and follow-through.

stances.label: control-sensitive
stances.description: Responsibility language can signal accountability, care, and follow-through.

expansion.paragraph: responsibility is represented here as a semantic-control term: its present usage and pragmatic force help decide whether an agent should ask, check evidence, avoid overclaiming, or revise before export.

trace.contribution: Ownership of a duty, effect, or response without necessarily making a legal admission.
Responsibility language can signal accountability, care, and follow-through.

capsule_summary: Semantic-control seed capsule for responsibility: Ownership of a duty, effect, or response without necessarily making a legal admission.
